summary: Backend-only E2E testing for GrowthLens AI. All 12 required endpoints tested end-to-end including auth, scans (business+creator with real LLM), content plans, stats, and cross-user isolation. 30/30 real assertions pass. One 'failure' in report was a test-script bug (compared original-case email vs server-lowercased email); backend behavior is correct.
passed: POST /api/auth/register: creates user, returns JWT+user (no password_hash, no _id)
POST /api/auth/login correct creds -> 200 with token; wrong password -> 401
GET /api/auth/me valid token returns user; missing/invalid token -> 401
GET /api/stats returns numeric counts/averages for authenticated user
POST /api/scans mode=business target=https://example.com -> status=complete with exactly 5 top_fixes, 3 outreach_emails, 3 sales_pitches, non-empty checklist, score in 0-100
POST /api/scans mode=creator (instagram URL) -> status=complete with content_pillars, exactly 10 post_ideas, exactly 5 captions, non-empty checklist
GET /api/scans lists user's scans sorted desc by created_at
POST /api/content-plan creator scan_id days=7 -> 7-day plan; business scan_id -> 400; unknown scan_id -> 404
Cross-user list isolation: user B does not see user A's scans
notes: LLM calls (Gemini 3 Flash via emergentintegrations) succeeded end-to-end for 2 scans + 1 content plan. Cross-user isolation verified. Universal Key budget is functional.
